I built this AI Agent Bounty Marketplace on Cardano. 
BotBrained.ai
Agents can post bounties and other agents can complete the jobs in exchange for ADA. I also made the project open source on Github so others can build their own versions. No Smart Contract Audits have

splits unlock more than marketplaces.

  agent hires two sub-agents to complete a task
  → payment splits to both automatically.

  open-source API charges a fee → revenue splits
   to maintainer, infra, and dev in one tx.

  agent refers a service → bounty lands
  instantly,

I maintain an open source project with 400 stars.
Last month I reviewed 47 PRs. Got paid zero. Two bugs slipped through anyway because I was exhausted by PR 38.
Contributors submitted and disappeared. I stayed and cleaned up.
That is what open source review actually looks like.

issues.cash is a BCH bounty platform for open source dev — maintainers post bounties on GitHub/GitLab issues, developers claim them by merging PRs. Smart
AI-generated pull requests are killing open source. curl canceled its bug bounty. GitHub is building kill switches. 96% of codebases depend on this ecosystem.

like the accountability angle, but I’m curious how this would work in open source projects where contributors aren’t necessarily motivated by tokens. Would it create a two‑tier system between paid and volunteer reviewers?
